signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry + Positioning + Headline
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5652. USD is the strongest G10 currency this week (+0.848% vs 7 peers) while NZD is the weakest (−1.310% vs 4 peers), creating a significant 2.158% spread. This divergence is driven by a hawkish Fed repricing, lifting the US Dollar against the NZD. USD also holds a +150bp carry advantage over NZD (SOFR 3.61% vs RBNZ 2.25%). NZD COT LF net short −22,454 (long%=18.4%) indicates extreme speculative short positioning, making the pair vulnerable to a short squeeze if RBNZ surprises with a hike. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 83% hike priced) on 8 Jul is the key catalyst.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Positioning + Volatility
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.703, trading above the 160.00 intervention watch level. The 263bp USD carry premium (SOFR 3.61% vs TONA 0.98%) continues to sustain the bid. JPY COT LF net short −112,092 (long%=29.7%) indicates crowded speculative short positioning, increasing the risk of a sharp unwind if BoJ intervenes or shifts policy. USD/JPY IV at 7.2% vs HV30 at 2.3% (VRP +4.9%) shows elevated options market demand for protection, with 25d RR at −1.47 indicating a moderate put skew. Intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 162.00; the next BoJ meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 31 Jul is critical.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6905. AUD COT LF net long +38,548 (long%=70.2%) indicates significant speculative long positioning, while the AUD CESI net −8 misses suggests data is consistently disappointing expectations. This COT-CESI divergence creates long-unwind risk. AUD/USD IV at 20.5% vs HV30 at 8.5% (VRP +12.0%) shows extremely elevated options market demand for protection, with 25d RR at −1.12 indicating a moderate put skew. Watch for a break below 0.6900 to confirm further downside pressure.

signals.title: USD/CHF — Hawkish Fed Bets + Carry + Volatility
signals.text: USD/CHF at 0.8116. The Swiss Franc has hit a ten-month low as hawkish Fed bets lift the US Dollar. USD holds a substantial +375bp carry advantage over CHF (Fed 3.75% vs SNB 0.0%). USD/CHF IV at 11.5% vs HV30 at 6.2% (VRP +5.3%) indicates elevated options market demand for protection, despite a mild call skew (25d RR +0.37). The SNB's next meeting on 24 Sep is a distant catalyst, leaving carry and Fed policy as primary drivers.
